cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The three great Islamic empires of the early modern era: Ottoman (Anatolia/Middle East), Safavid (Persia), and Mughal (India)

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Islamic empire that ruled from Anatolia, capturing Constantinople in 1453 and expanding into Europe, the Middle East, and North Africa

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Suleiman the Magnificent
cards.answer: Ottoman Sultan (r. 1520–1566) who expanded the empire to its greatest extent, reformed legal codes, and patronized arts and architecture

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Ottoman practice of taking Christian boys from conquered territories, converting them to Islam, and training them as elite soldiers or administrators

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Elite Ottoman infantry soldiers originally recruited through the devshirme system

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Ottoman administrative system that allowed non-Muslim communities (Christians, Jews) to govern their own religious affairs

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Persian Shia Muslim empire that clashed with the Ottoman Sunni Empire over both territory and Islamic religious authority

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Shah Abbas I
cards.answer: Safavid ruler (r. 1588–1629) who strengthened the empire, reformed the military, and made Isfahan a magnificent capital

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The major division within Islam that divided Sunni and Shia, a key source of conflict between the Ottomans and Safavids

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Muslim empire founded by Babur that ruled most of the Indian subcontinent from 1526 to the 18th century

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Akbar the Great
cards.answer: Mughal Emperor (r. 1556–1605) known for religious tolerance, administrative reforms, and expanding the empire across most of India

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Mughal policy of religious tolerance and syncretism under Akbar that sought to unite Hindus and Muslims

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Mughal land revenue system dividing the empire into districts administered by appointed officials who collected taxes

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Aurangzeb
cards.answer: Mughal Emperor (r. 1658–1707) who reversed Akbar's religious tolerance, imposed Islamic law, banned Hindu temples, and expanded but ultimately destabilized the empire
alternate_answers: Mughal emperor who undermined religious tolerance

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Russian empire that expanded across Siberia to the Pacific, incorporating diverse indigenous peoples into an Orthodox Christian state

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Peter the Great
cards.answer: Russian Tsar (r. 1682–1725) who modernized Russia by adopting Western European technology, culture, and administrative practices

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Chinese dynasty (1368–1644) that expelled the Mongols and rebuilt the Great Wall and Grand Canal while initially welcoming trade

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Zheng He
cards.answer: Ming Dynasty admiral who led seven large naval expeditions (1405–1433) across the Indian Ocean to Southeast Asia, India, Arabia, and East Africa

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Zheng He's voyages across the Indian Ocean

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Chinese policy of restricting overseas trade and contact under the late Ming and early Qing dynasties

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Manchurian dynasty that replaced the Ming in China in 1644, ruling until 1912, and required Han Chinese men to wear the queue hairstyle as a sign of submission

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Aztec Triple Alliance that dominated Mesoamerica through tribute extraction and military conquest before Spanish arrival

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The economic theory that colonies exist to enrich the mother country by providing raw materials and serving as a market for manufactured goods

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Absolute monarchy
cards.answer: A system of government in which a king or queen holds all political power, unchecked by laws or representative assemblies, as seen in Louis XIV's France

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Louis XIV
cards.answer: French absolute monarch (r. 1643–1715) who centralized power, built Versailles, and declared 'L'état, c'est moi' (I am the state)

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A political theory developed in 17th-century England that limited royal power through Parliament and individual rights

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Ottoman official who served as the Sultan's chief minister and managed the day-to-day administration of the empire

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Beginning of the Mughal Empire with Babur's conquest of Delhi

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Ottoman navy's defeat at the Battle of Lepanto that ended Ottoman naval dominance in the Mediterranean

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Battle of Lepanto

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Suleiman the Magnificent
cards.answer: Ottoman sultan (r. 1520–1566) who expanded Ottoman territory to its greatest extent and presided over the height of Ottoman cultural and administrative power

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Ottoman system of recruiting Christian boys from the Balkans, converting them to Islam, and training them as elite soldiers and administrators

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Elite Ottoman infantry soldiers who used firearms and formed the core of the Ottoman army

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Ottoman conquest of Constantinople, ending the Byzantine Empire

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Ottoman administrative system that allowed religious minorities (Christians, Jews) to govern themselves according to their own laws in personal matters

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Shah Ismail I
cards.answer: Founded the Safavid Dynasty in 1501 and declared Twelver Shia Islam the state religion of Persia, creating a lasting Sunni-Shia divide with the Ottoman Empire

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Battle of Chaldiran: Ottomans defeat Safavids, demonstrating Ottoman military superiority

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Akbar the Great
cards.answer: Mughal Emperor (r. 1556–1605) who expanded the empire across most of South Asia and adopted a policy of religious tolerance to unify his diverse subjects

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Mughal salary-based system of granting administrative ranks (mansabs) to officials who were paid in cash and required to maintain cavalry

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Mughal emperor renowned for constructing the Taj Mahal and known for reversing Akbar's religious tolerance

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Aurangzeb
cards.answer: Last major Mughal emperor (r. 1658–1707) who reversed his predecessors' policies of religious tolerance, reimposed the jizya tax on non-Muslims, and expanded the empire to its greatest but most unstable extent

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Zheng He's first voyage from China across the Indian Ocean

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Qing Dynasty's policy of requiring Han Chinese men to adopt the Manchu hairstyle as a sign of submission to their rule

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Ming Dynasty is established, overthrowing Mongol Yuan rule in China

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The idea that the political authority of European monarchs came directly from God and could not be challenged by subjects or church

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Peter the Great
cards.answer: Russian tsar (r. 1682–1725) who westernized Russia's military, government, and culture by importing European technology and practices, transforming Russia into a major European power

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Peace of Westphalia ends the Thirty Years War and establishes the principle of state sovereignty

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Russian term for serfs tied to the land owned by nobles, whose labor the state increasingly controlled to support the military and economy

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Martin Luther's 1517 challenge to the Catholic Church's authority that sparked the Protestant Reformation and the fragmentation of Western Christianity

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A Chinese-style script created for the Korean language at the order of King Sejong, designed to increase literacy among common people
